mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate operating leverage, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity prices align with realized earnings growth.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns following months with rising INDPRO fail to exceed the unconditional 65.5% baseline by at least 5 percentage points over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates short-term real interest rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated unemployment and restrictive short-term interest rates indicate a late-cycle liquidity squeeze where labor market deterioration coincides with tight credit; the joint condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via collapsing corporate margins and falling discount factor cash flows.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% and FEDFUNDS at 5.02% as of 2007-09-15
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of joint-trigger instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term fails to add marginal explanatory power beyond the main effects (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a rising unemployment rate signals the erosion of household income growth and the onset of recessionary dynamics; an increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in more than 50% of triggered late-cycle observations, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not below -0.2 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in new private housing starts exceeding 5% signals a contraction in residential investment that transmits to construction employment and mortgage credit losses, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns follow fewer than 40% of month-over-month HOUST declines exceeding 5%, or the signal Rank IC is not negative (Rank IC > -0.02) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects accelerating consumer spending velocity and revenue top-line surprises for consumer-facing equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS momentum do not exceed the unconditional baseline positive rate by at least 3 percentage points over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing retail sales growth signals involuntary inventory accumulation and impending production cutbacks; the interaction between INDPRO and RSAFS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as margins compress during destocking.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-RSAFS interaction model does not produce a negative Rank IC with magnitude exceeding 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant (t-stat > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year erodes real disposable income and forces equity risk premia higher, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered months, or the mean return conditional on CPIAUCSL year-over-year above 2.5% is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
